generatedSentence: Elara, an affable economist, easily dismissed the anxiety that often plagued her. Her daddy, a retired soldier with a single earlock, always preached resilience from his ergonomic armchair, often after making pancakes. This morning, a lucky lottery ticket from her inbox, earmarked for a new land trust, offered an uplifting perspective, erasing any lingering worries about overdue loan repayments or unfair partnerships; it was a marvelous opportunity to install a new future, rather than retrace old, regrettable steps. She absolutely knew this prize held the capacity to change everything, promising a life less burdened by past suffering and more grounded in hope, a chance to escape her perpetually cranky disposition. But a troubling update arrived: a shadowy broker enabled by tribalistic raiders had initiated a replevy action, seeking extradition for a rare genetic experiment, a platypus-hybrid rumored to possess unique hormone-driven capabilities. Vanishing evidence and the ghost of a bloodbath suggested a ruthless organization at play. Elara, though plucky, felt the drag of this new conflict, watching a lone trolleybus pass a highrise, knowing her comfortable life was tainted. This was not the alternative she envisioned; the threat of a full-scale slaughter loomed, an enticement for them to surrender the platypus, a creature they considered nothing more than a dummy for their anti-human agenda. Determined, Elara donned a disguise, a tactical suit the color of lavender, and quickly teleports North, her coiffeur's skilled hands having prepared her head for combat. She outjumped a series of electric elevators, the 'boing' of her specialized boots echoing through the sterile hallways of the raiders' compound, heading towards the coffer where they kept their prize. A nitwit guard, unable to comprehend her speed, suffered a mighty blow as she efficiently bypassed him. She used a cylinder filled with glycerine and neem to disable a tripwire, then engaged a wolf-like enforcer, rewind capabilities allowing her to dodge blows like a roadrunner, all while admiring her own tactical genius. Through the overhead opera of alarms and the harsh tones of their security systems, Elara chucked a bouquet of azaleas into the sewage vent, signaling her victory as she released the platypus. The creature, a wonder of evolution, gave her kudos with a gentle 'cuppy' nudge. She knew her actions were mortal, yet she felt no need to forgive her adversaries for their greed. The deed was done; she had ensured this rare life's freedom, leaving behind only the rubber remnants of her disguise and a half-eaten tomato and pickles sandwich, a small appendix to a grand, elegant rescue. The wallpaper in the nearby kindergarten would remain safe, no longer threatened by the raiders' masculine, rockfish-like security chief.

This morning, a lucky lottery ticket from her inbox, earmarked for a new land trust, offered an uplifting perspective, erasing any lingering worries about overdue loan repayments or unfair partnerships; it was a marvelous opportunity to install a new future, rather than retrace old, regrettable steps. She absolutely knew this prize held the capacity to change everything, promising a life less burdened by past suffering and more grounded in hope, a chance to escape her perpetually cranky disposition.

But a troubling update arrived: a shadowy broker enabled by tribalistic raiders had initiated a replevy action, seeking extradition for a rare genetic experiment, a platypus-hybrid rumored to possess unique hormone-driven capabilities. Elara, though plucky, felt the drag of this new conflict, watching a lone trolleybus pass a highrise, knowing her comfortable life was tainted. This was not the alternative she envisioned; the threat of a full-scale slaughter loomed, an enticement for them to surrender the platypus, a creature they considered nothing more than a dummy for their anti-human agenda.

imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist: A cybernetically enhanced human-platypus hybrid, wearing a luminous teal-and-silver combat suit and a high-tech data monocle. TOP-LEFT PANEL: The platypus-hybrid protagonist, 'Elara', sits casually on an ergonomic armchair in a futuristic, clean apartment, looking at a glowing lottery ticket held in her webbed hand, a holographic interface hovering nearby, full shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Elara stands in front of a sleek, neon-lit highrise window, observing a distant, menacing shadow of a trolleybus passing, a worried expression on her face as a red alert icon flashes on her data monocle, medium shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Elara, in her combat suit, teleports with a 'boing' effect, leaping over a series of glowing electric elevators in a stylized futuristic building, dynamic low-angle shot, action focused on her movement. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Elara and the platypus creature stand together, looking out at a vibrant, liberated landscape of azaleas and clean skies, the highrise now in the background, a sense of peace and triumph on their faces, wide shot. NO text, NO speech bubbles, NO dialogue.

definitions.definition: An act of going out or going away, or leaving; a departure.
definitions.example: He made his exit at the opportune time.

definitions.definition: A way out.
The act of departing from life; death.
definitions.example: the untimely exit of a respected politician

definitions.definition: To go out or go away from a place or situation; to depart, to leave.
To depart from life; to die.
To end or terminate (a program, subroutine, etc.)
(originally United States) To depart from or leave (a place or situation).
Income, returns, revenue.
A mark or deformation of the ear of an animal, intended to indicate ownership.
The designation of specific projects in appropriations of funding for general programs.
A mark for identification; a distinguishing mark.
To mark (as of sheep) by slitting the ear.
(by extension) To specify or set aside for a particular purpose, to allocate.
definitions.example: You can donate to the organization as a whole, or you can earmark your contribution for a particular project.

definitions.definition: Comfortably, without discomfort or anxiety.
Without difficulty.
definitions.example: Individuals without a family network are easily controlled.

definitions.definition: Absolutely, without question.
definitions.example: This is easily the best meal I have eaten.

definitions.definition: A lock of curly hair worn by the ear, often by Jewish men for religious reasons, and formerly by Elizabethan dandies.
A scheme for the distribution of prizes by lot or chance, especially a gaming scheme in which one or more tickets bearing particular numbers draw prizes, the other tickets are blanks.
An affair of chance.
(Shakespeare) Allotment; a thing allotted.
A widely cultivated plant, Solanum lycopersicum, having edible fruit.
The savory fruit of this plant, red when ripe, treated as a vegetable in horticulture and cooking.
A shade of red, the colour of a ripe tomato.
A desirable-looking woman.
definitions.example: Lookit the legs on that hot tomato!

definitions.definition: A stupid act or person.
To pelt with tomatoes
To add tomatoes to (a dish)
A bus, powered via overhead electric cables, that does not run on tracks
A plant of the obsolete genus Azalea.
A chair with supports for the arms or elbows.
To create based on theory or general knowledge rather than data.
To theorize based on analysis of data that was gathered previously; to reflect.
Remote from actual involvement, including a person retired from previously active involvement.
definitions.example: These days I'm an armchair detective.

definitions.definition: Unqualified or uninformed but yet giving advice, especially on technical issues, such as law, architecture, medicine, military theory, or sports.
definitions.example: After the American football game, the armchair quarterbacks talked about what they would have done differently to win, if they had been star athletes instead of out-of-shape old men.

definitions.definition: Replevin
To return goods to their rightful owner by replevin; to recover goods.
To bail.
(usually uncountable) Any of a large number of different species of fish, which dwell among rocks, specifically:
A black person who does not know how to swim.
Pliable material derived from the sap of the rubber tree; a hydrocarbon polymer of isoprene.
Synthetic materials with the same properties as natural rubber.
An eraser.
A condom.
Someone or something which rubs.
The cushion of an electric machine.
The rectangular pad on the pitcher's mound from which the pitcher must pitch.
definitions.example: Jones toes the rubber and then fires to the plate.

definitions.definition: (in the plural) Water-resistant shoe covers, galoshes, overshoes.
definitions.example: Johnny, don't forget your rubbers today.

definitions.definition: Tires, particularly racing tires.
definitions.example: Jones enters the pits to get new rubber.

definitions.definition: A hardship or misfortune.
(of a draft/check) Not covered by funds on account.
Material (such as clothing, makeup, a wig) used to alter one’s visual appearance in order to hide one's identity or assume another.
definitions.example: A cape and moustache completed his disguise.

definitions.definition: The appearance of something on the outside which masks what's beneath.
The act of disguising, notably as a ploy.
definitions.example: Any disguise may expose soldiers to be deemed enemy spies.

definitions.definition: A change of behaviour resulting from intoxication.
To change the appearance of (a person or thing) so as to hide, or to assume an identity.
definitions.example: Spies often disguise themselves.

definitions.definition: To avoid giving away or revealing (something secret); to hide by a false appearance.
definitions.example: He disguised his true intentions.

definitions.definition: To affect or change by liquor; to intoxicate.
A silent person; a person who does not talk.
An unintelligent person.
definitions.example: Don't be such a dummy!

definitions.definition: A figure of a person or animal used by a ventriloquist; a puppet.
Something constructed with the size and form of a human, to be used in place of a person.
definitions.example: To understand the effects of the accident, we dropped a dummy from the rooftop.

definitions.definition: A person who is the mere tool of another; a man of straw.
A deliberately nonfunctional device or tool used in place of a functional one.
definitions.example: The hammer and drill in the display are dummies.

definitions.definition: A "dummy teat"; a plastic or rubber teat used to soothe or comfort a baby; a pacifier.
definitions.example: The baby wants her dummy.

definitions.definition: A player whose hand is shown and is to be played from by another player.
A word serving only to make a construction grammatical.
definitions.example: The pronoun "it" in "It's a mystery why this happened" is a dummy.

definitions.definition: An unused parameter or value.
definitions.example: If flag1 is false, the other parameters are dummies.

definitions.definition: A feigned pass or kick or play in order to deceive an opponent.
A bodily gesture meant to fool an opposing player; a feint.
To make a mock-up or prototype version of something, without some or all off its intended functionality.
definitions.example: The carpenters dummied some props for the rehearsals.

definitions.definition: To feint.
A member of an army, of any rank.
A private in military service, as distinguished from an officer.
A guardsman.
A member of the Salvation Army.
A piece of buttered bread (or toast), cut into a long thin strip for dipping into a soft-boiled egg.
A term of affection for a young boy.
Someone who fights or toils well.
The red or cuckoo gurnard (Chelidonichthys cuculus).
One of the asexual polymorphic forms of termites, in which the head and jaws are very large and strong. The soldiers serve to defend the nest.
A red herring (cured kipper with flesh turned red).
To continue steadfast; to keep striving.
To serve as a soldier.
To intentionally restrict labor productivity; to work at the slowest rate that goes unpunished.
To take a ride on (another person's horse) without permission.
A thin batter cake fried in a pan or on a griddle in oil or butter.
A kind of makeup, consisting of a thick layer of a compressed powder.
A type of throw, usually with a ring where the prop is thrown in such a way that it rotates round an axis of the diameter of the prop.
Anything very thin and flat.
A box on which an actor stands to make them appear taller.

A semi-aquatic, egg-laying monotreme mammal with a bill resembling that of a duck, that has a mole-like body, a tail resembling that of a beaver, a waterproof pelt, and flat webbed feet — males have poisonous spurs on the inside of the back legs; Ornithorhynchus anatinus
Relating to genetics or genes.
Caused by genes.
Of or relating to origin (genesis).
Based on shared membership in a linguistic family.
definitions.example: Chinese has borrowed several words from English, but it does not have a genetic relationship to English.

definitions.definition: Based on a shared membership in a religious family.
To become invisible or to move out of view unnoticed.
To become equal to zero.
definitions.example: The function f(x)=x^2 vanishes at x=0.

definitions.definition: To disappear; to kidnap
That vanishes.
Facts or observations presented in support of an assertion.
definitions.example: There is no evidence that anyone was here earlier.

definitions.definition: Anything admitted by a court to prove or disprove alleged matters of fact in a trial.
One who bears witness.
A body of objectively verifiable facts that are positively indicative of, and/or exclusively concordant with, that one conclusion over any other.

definitions.definition: An expert in economics, especially one who studies economic data and extracts higher-level information or proposes theories.
One concerned with political economy.
One who manages a household.
One who economizes, or manages domestic or other concerns with frugality; one who expends money, time, or labor, judiciously, and without waste.
In an efficient manner.
Indiscriminate killing or slaughter, a massacre.
An aggressive or very violent contest or confrontation.
An upset (as of a game with unexpected results, or a national presidential convention) or heavy defeat.
A large financial loss or massive layoff brought about by negative economic conditions.
A bath taken in warm blood used as a restorative or medical treatment.
A bunch of cut flowers.
definitions.example: For my birthday I received two bouquets.

definitions.definition: The scent of a particular wine.
definitions.example: This Bordeaux has an interesting bouquet.

definitions.definition: The heart note of a perfume.
definitions.example: The remarkable flower bouquet lasts for hours until it dissolves into a sweet vanilla smell.

definitions.definition: A compliment or expression of praise.
A bouquet of circles.
The reserve of cards in the game of Flower Garden and variations.
The ninth Lenormand card, sometimes called Flowers instead.
The expense of a business not directly assigned to goods or services provided.
The items or classes of expense not directly assigned to goods or services provided.
Any cost or expenditure (monetary, time, effort or otherwise) incurred in a project or activity, which does not directly contribute to the progress or outcome of the project or activity.
Wasted money.
A smash.
The ceiling of any enclosed space below decks in a vessel.
A compartment above the seats for stowing luggage in a passenger aircraft.
The system of overhead wires used to power electric transport, such as streetcars, trains, or buses.
Data or steps of computation used only to facilitate the computations in the system and not directly related to the actual program code or data being processed.
definitions.example: Network overhead is the header data that is required to route and transport data over network, whereas fork overhead is the additional time and memory cost of creating and managing new processes within operating system.

definitions.definition: (by ellipsis) An overhead throw.
Located above, especially over the head

definitions.definition: Kicked over one's own head
Above one's head; in the sky.

definitions.definition: Per head; for each individual.
An unpleasant state of mental uneasiness, nervousness, apprehension and obsession or concern about some uncertain event.
An uneasy or distressing desire (for something).
A state of restlessness and agitation, often accompanied by a distressing sense of oppression or tightness in the stomach.
Receiving others kindly and conversing with them in a free and friendly manner; friendly, courteous, sociable.
Mild; benign.

Do you want a free cookie with that coffee?
Absolutely!

definitions.definition: To establish or settle in.

The state of being associated with a partner.
An association of two or more people to conduct a business,

definitions.definition: Having the form of a cup.
Having indentations or shallow depressions.
(of timber) Characterised by cup shakes.
The ability to hold, receive or absorb

The maximum amount that can be held

definitions.definition: Capability; the ability to perform some task
The maximum that can be produced.

A faculty; the potential for growth and development
A role; the position in which one functions
Legal authority (to make an arrest for example)
Electrical capacitance.
(operations) The maximum that can be produced on a machine or in a facility or group.

definitions.definition: Filling the allotted space.

definitions.definition: Decorative paper-like material used to cover the inner walls of buildings.

A style or design of such material.

Generic footage of a building or location that may be used in the background of a related news report.
Gaudy mass-produced stamps, typically of little or no value and only produced for sale to collectors.
A radio operator's collection of awards and QSL cards.
To cover (a wall, a room, etc) with wallpaper.

definitions.definition: A man who makes amorous advances to many women.
A wolf tone or wolf note.

definitions.definition: Any very ravenous, rapacious, or destructive person or thing; especially, want; starvation.

A white worm, or maggot, which infests granaries.
An eating ulcer or sore. See lupus.
A willying machine.
To devour; to gobble; to eat (something) voraciously.

To hunt for wolves.

A device (e.g. a jumar) which will slide along a rope in one direction but not the other, used in rock-climbing, caving etc.

definitions.definition: Producing, or resulting in, good fortune

Having a pale purple colour.

definitions.definition: To wind (something) again.

To go back or think back to a previous moment or place, or a previous point in a discourse.

definitions.definition: To give a basic education in a particular subject; to instruct in elements or first principles.

definitions.definition: To hit a ground ball. Compare fly (verb(regular)) and line (verb).

To run aground; to strike the bottom and remain fixed.

definitions.definition: To found; to fix or set, as on a foundation, reason, or principle; to furnish a ground for; to fix firmly.
To cover with a ground, as a copper plate for etching, or as paper or other materials with a uniform tint as a preparation for ornament.
To improve or focus the mental or emotional state of.
definitions.example: I ground myself with meditation.

definitions.definition: (of an airman) Not allowed to fly.

(of a person) Mature, sensible with well-considered priorities.
Of or pertaining to an electrical conductor which is connected to earth; earthed.

The act or practice of enticing, of alluring or tempting
That which entices, or incites to evil; means of allurement; an alluring object
Praise; accolades.

definitions.definition: Credit for one's achievements.
To pardon; to waive any negative feeling or desire for punishment, retribution, or compensation.
definitions.example: Forgive others, not because they deserve forgiveness, but because you deserve peace.

Exciting wonder or surprise; astonishing; wonderful; delightful.
definitions.example: I went to a marvelous party last week.

definitions.definition: A tall building, one of many stories.
To undergo hardship.

definitions.definition: To feel pain.
definitions.example: At least he didn't suffer when he died in the car crash.

definitions.definition: To become worse.
definitions.example: If you keep partying like this, your school-work will suffer.

definitions.definition: To endure, undergo.
definitions.example: I've been suffering your insults for years.

definitions.definition: To allow.

To bore or turn (a hole) in a revolving piece held in a chuck.
To make a clucking sound.
To call, as a hen her chickens.
To touch or tap gently.

To discard, to throw away.

definitions.definition: To jilt; to dump.
definitions.example: She's chucked me for another man!

definitions.definition: To vomit.
To leave; to depart; to bounce.
definitions.example: Let's chuck.

definitions.definition: To chuckle; to laugh.
On rhythm guitar or mandolin etc.: to mute a chord by lifting the fretting fingers immediately after strumming, producing a percussive effect.
A strong chest or box used for keeping money or valuables safe.
An ornamental sunken panel in a ceiling or dome.
A cofferdam.
A supply or store of money, often belonging to an organization.
A trench dug in the bottom of a dry moat, and extending across it, to enable the besieged to defend it with raking fire.
